---
title: "Testing git again"
author: "Ruhil"
date: "2026-04-13"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  cache = TRUE, fig.retina = 3, fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 9, fig.height = 6, dev = "svg", out.width = "100%"
  )
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}

library(tidyverse)

diamonds %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = carat,
      y = price,
      color = cut
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ color)


```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}

library(highcharter)
data(weather)

x <- c("Min", "Mean", "Max")
y <- sprintf("{point.%s}°", c("min_temperaturec", "mean_temperaturec", "max_temperaturec"))

tltip <- tooltip_table(x, y)

hchart(
  weather,
  type = "columnrange",
  hcaes(
    x = date, 
    low = min_temperaturec, 
    high = max_temperaturec,
    color = mean_temperaturec
    )
  ) |>
  hc_chart(
    polar = TRUE
    ) |>
  hc_yAxis(
    max = 30,
    min = -10,
    labels = list(format = "{value} C"),
    showFirstLabel = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_xAxis(
    title = list(text = ""), 
    gridLineWidth = 0.5,
    labels = list(format = "{value: %b}")
  ) |>
  hc_tooltip(
    useHTML = TRUE,
    pointFormat = tltip,
    headerFormat = as.character(tags$small("{point.x:%d %B, %Y}"))
  ) |> 
  hc_title(
    text = "Climatical characteristics of San Francisco"
  ) |> 
  hc_size(
    height = 600
  )

```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
